_comment: Copy this file to fleet.json and edit — fleet.json is gitignored (it's your machine's private grants). secrets_root is a shared CATALOG of reusable entries (credential files + .env fragments), referenced by relative path; isolation happens here in the grant, so a box sees only what its entry lists and can never get more. projects_root is where your repos live; each repo's `path` is relative to it.

myapp._note: `allow` is the trust gate — gent refuses to start a box whose entry isn't allow:true. This example grants a scoped GCP service-account key (mounted read-only), a shared .env fragment, a read-only per-owner GitHub token (so in-box `gh` can read PRs/diffs/CI — see README), and some inline ids.

minimal._note: Simplest case: a repo in its own box, no secrets, no env.
